name: Topp Racing
bio: Championship-leading team through two rounds of the 2026 season. Topp Racing runs Tyler Maxson — the Pro class points leader — alongside Rookie Cole Kleck. Both swept Sebring from pole position, and Maxson has yet to finish lower than fourth. One of the most formidable two-car lineups in the Pro field.

name: Kellymoss
bio: The largest operation in the PCCNA paddock. Kellymoss runs entries across all three classes in 2026 — Aaron Jeansonne leads the Pro effort at P2 in the championship, while the Pro-Am and Masters squads add depth. The Wisconsin-based Porsche specialist has decades of customer racing experience behind every car on the grid.

name: JDX Racing
bio: One of PCCNA's most decorated customer operations. JDX fields Callum Hedge — P3 in the 2026 Pro standings — alongside regulars Connor Bloum and Sabre Cook in Pro, and Kevin Stadtlander in Masters. JDX's engineering depth gives every driver a title-ready machine from round one.

name: ACI Motorsports
bio: A large, well-funded customer racing outfit covering every class in 2026. ACI Motorsports fields Patrick Mulcahy — the Pro-Am championship leader — alongside multiple Pro entries and Marco Cirone leading the Masters standings. The team is a fixture at every IMSA support event on the calendar.

name: GMG Racing
bio: Florida-based Porsche customer team founded by James Sofronas. GMG struck gold at Long Beach with Tom Sargent sweeping both races — the Australian Porsche factory driver arrived late to the 2026 season and immediately went to the top of the results. Sofronas himself races the Pro-Am entry and JCD Dubets rounds out the Pro effort.

name: JTR Motorsports Engineering
bio: Focused, engineering-led Porsche customer team. Jared Thomas runs a competitive Pro effort — he qualified on pole at Sebring and finished second in Race 1. Rob Walker runs the Masters entry alongside him, and JTR's technical approach gives both drivers a data-rich environment to find time.

name: CSM - Reis Nichols
bio: Czabok-Simpson Motorsport — running as CSM Reis Nichols in 2026 — fields Nikita Lastochkin in the Pro class. The team is a compact single-car operation focused on developing the Russian driver's pace in the North American series environment.

name: Ruckus Racing
bio: Scott Blind's single-car Masters programme. Blind won the Masters class at Miami in 2025 — his home-track knowledge makes Ruckus Racing one to watch this weekend. The team runs lean and fast, with the sole ambition of a Masters championship title.

name: van der Steur Racing
bio: Dutch-American customer racing squad with a long IMSA history. van der Steur's PCCNA entry runs Brady Behrman in the Pro-Am class — a well-prepared car from a team that knows how to win in the one-make format.

name: Scalzo Autosport
bio: Owner-driver operation run by Alain Scalzo himself. Scalzo Autosport is the purest expression of the gentleman-racer ethos — one driver, one car, one goal. The team handles its own logistics, its own data, and its own motivation.
